defect.finding: The authority normalizer used an unbounded substring test for the word press, so Express Bus and Expressway text could be classified as press_release.
defect.impact: Three MTA staff summaries, one NYC DOT presentation, and the newly curated structured schedule query carried false press_release tiers; many other staff summaries lacked the board_material companion entirely.

rule_change.bare_press_signal: Require a complete normalized token for bare press, while retaining explicit press_release, announcement, and newsroom signals.
rule_change.precedence_guard: Keep genuine press-release signals ahead of dataset signals so a press release that discusses an open-data dataset remains press_release.
rule_change.dataset_fallthrough: A structured dataset description containing express but no complete press token falls through to dataset_documentation.

regression_guards: staff summary titled Express Bus Service Additions -> board_material
structured dataset query describing express trips -> dataset_documentation
press release announcing an open-data dataset -> press_release

canonical_sweep.interpretation: The 65 null-to-board changes are an intentional corpus-wide staff-summary/committee-material backfill. The four existing false positives and one new schedule-query false positive require guarded semantic corrections because their accepted journals already contain the stale tier.
